highlights.text: This got us long, and its why I wrote on Monday at 4pm (when we were 5160s): “My general lean is ES can try to backtest 5231, then 5248-52….
I wrote on Wednesday at 4pm that: “My general lean is we can fill out the 5377/87 to 5475 range more.
My guess was yes, and I wrote yesterday: “ My general lean is to defer to the trend.
All major short squeezes in ES are caused by Failed Breakdowns, which in turn, occur after deep, “scary” legs lower.
Monday afternoon however, ES put in a monster Failed Breakdown of the 5155 level which was a 1.5 week low set April 10th.
Then yesterday, another Failed Breakdown would take us for another leg up.
Early Thursday morning, we flushed it by 20 points and recovered (Failed Breakdown).
Is “buy the dip” back for good?

In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into 1) Some of the quality long setups we had on yesterday at 5377 (including the Failed Breakdown that triggered the long that lasted into today and 2) I’ll talk how I trail my stops and use runners.

post_title: After 4 Green Days In A Row, Is "Buy The Dip" Here To Stay? Not So Fast. April 28 Plan
